turns.head: Hi, I would like you to work on this excursion that has been
turns.full: Hi, I would like you to work on this excursion that has been scoped but not otherwise started: /home/joe/code/futon2/holes/E-precision-over-policies.md

turns.head: So I will ask you to do all the building directly this time
turns.full: So I will ask you to do all the building directly this time rather than bell codex.  As for your questions: It sounds like for (2) you did the correct research.  For (3), claude-10 is working on R16 so this may be a case for pairing with them to agree on an interface.  The HTML explainer might need to expand with a bit more information about interfaces (e.g. round nodes on the edges that can be opened for details).  If you need to use a short whistle salvo to coordinate with claude-10 that's fine

turns.head: Please continue we ran into a usage limitation but should be
turns.full: Please continue we ran into a usage limitation but should be able to pick up now

turns.head: Now R14/γ is committed — can you give a quick deterministic
turns.full: Now R14/γ is committed — can you give a quick deterministic demonstration that γ actually self-calibrates: a run of policies whose realized outcomes beat expectation should sharpen selection (γ↑ / τ↓), and a run that underperforms should hedge (γ↓)? A small REPL check against the new code is enough.

turns.head: Performance reading is correct — it matches the excursion HE
turns.full: Performance reading is correct — it matches the excursion HEAD ("confidence in its own decision-making, inferred from realized-vs-expected outcomes"). Lock it in: add a regression test that pins γ self-calibration (sharpen on a run of good outcomes, hedge on a run of bad), so it cannot silently drift, then R14 is closed.
